name: Self-update from GitHub Releases
description: Sideloaded VSIX updater. Compares the installed extension version against the latest GitHub release tag, downloads the .vsix asset to a temp dir, installs it via workbench.extensions.installExtension, and offers a window reload. Exposed as the codeWorkbench.checkForUpdates command (Workbench brand view title-bar button + palette) plus a once-per-day silent check on activation. When an update exists, applyUpdateState() persists the tag, flips the codeWorkbench.updateAvailable context key (title-bar icon swaps cloud-download → arrow-circle-up) and sets a badge on the brand view.
guidelines: Release tags are v<semver> and the asset is code-workbench-<semver>.vsix; release.yml bumps package.json on main so installed version == tag.
Keep the activation check throttled (globalState timestamp) — unauthenticated GitHub API allows 60 req/h per IP.
Silent mode must never surface errors or up-to-date toasts; only the manual command talks on failure.
Vitest imports src modules in a node env: 'vscode' is aliased to test/stubs/vscode.ts. Extend the stub rather than mocking per-test.
applyUpdateState() is the ONLY writer of the availableVersion key, the updateAvailable context key and the brand-view badge — keep them in lockstep by routing every set/clear through it.
The brand view is lazy: BrandViewProvider.setBadge() stores the badge and resolveWebviewView() re-applies it, so a badge set during activation survives first reveal.
Context keys and badges do not survive a window reload — registerUpdateCommand() replays the persisted tag on activation.
anti_patterns: Do not check for updates on every activation — it burns the API rate limit and nags the user.
Do not hardcode the repo from packageJSON.repository; that URL still points at the old monorepo path.
Do not set view.badge directly from outside BrandViewProvider — a badge assigned before the view resolves is silently dropped.
decisions: Ship via GitHub Releases + self-update rather than the VS Code Marketplace / Open VSX, which would give native updates but require publisher accounts.
'Skip This Version' is persisted in globalState and cleared whenever the user runs the manual check; skipping also clears the update-available icon and badge.
The title-bar icon swap is done with two when-gated menus.view/title entries on the same command rather than a second command, so the palette keeps one entry.
